content.problemStatement: A manufacturing plant wants to calculate the total number of even parts produced in a day. Given that the part numbers range from `1 to a predefined number n`, your task is to write a Python program that uses the accumulator pattern in a while loop to sum all the even numbers from `1 to n`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100000) – the upper bound of the range 1..n.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of all even numbers from 1 to n.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For n=1 there are no even numbers, sum=0. For n=5 the evens are 2 and 4, sum=6. For n=10 the evens are 2,4,6,8,10, sum=30.
n=2 → only even number 2, sum=2. n=7 → evens 2,4,6, sum=12.
Even numbers up to 15 are 2,4,6,8,10,12,14; their sum is 56.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate from 1 to n using a while loop. An accumulator variable sum_even stores the running total. Inside the loop we check if the current counter is even (counter % 2 == 0); if so we add it to sum_even. After the loop finishes we output sum_even. This follows the classic accumulator pattern and works within the given limits because the total number of iterations in the worst case is 100 * 100000 = 10⁷, which easily fits in time limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T · n) in the worst case (each test case scans all numbers up to n).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
